english: luptuousness, = ₒdod - čágs ('dod chags), frq.; meton., coitus, ₒdód - pa spyód - pa ('dod pa spyod pa) to practise it; ₒdód-pai dus ₒdébs-pa ('dod pai dus 'debs pa) to agree upon the time for cohabiting Tar. - 2. Ssk. इच्छा, a wish, ₒdód - pa γsum γnán - na ('dod pa gsum gnan na) if three wishes are granted Dzl.; meton. the object of desire, ₒdód - pa tób - pa ('dod pa thob pa); ₒdód - pa daṅ ₒbrál-ba ('dod pa dang 'bral ba) to be separated from the object of one's desire. - 3. supposition Tar. 45, 21. - 4. W. semen virile. - 5. Kama, Cupid, the god of love and of lust. - 6. symb. num.: 13. Comp. ₒdod-Káms ('dod khams) the world of sensual pleasure, the world of Brahma; ₒDod-Kams-bdág-ma ('dod khams bdag ma), prop. n. = Skye-dgui-bdág-mo (skye dgui bdag mo), = Dpal-lhá-mo (dpal lha mo). - ₒdód-mKan ('dod mkhan) he that wishes, seeks, sues, a lover, suitor, cca., nai bu-mo ₒdód-mKan mán-po ₒdug (nai bu mo 'dod mkhan mang po 'dug) there are here many suitors of my daughter Glr. - ₒdod-dgu ('dod dgu) all wishes, lus ₒdod-dgúr sgyúr-ba (lus 'dod dgur sgyur ba) to transform one's self at pleasure Mil., Stg. - ₒdód-čan ('dod can), ₒdod-ldán ('dod ldan), ₒdód-pa-čan ('dod pa can) eager, desirous Cs. - ₒdod - čágs (राग) ('dod chags) passion, carnal desire, lust, frq., ₒdod-čágs skyés-te ('dod chags skyes te), ₒdod-čágs-kyis gdúns-te ('dod chags kyis gdungs te); as the highest of the three guṇa (cf. gtí-mug (gti mug)) it corresponds to सत्त्व, virtue, and is symbolized as cock or hen, though Tibetan readers probably never understand anything else by it than sensual indulgence. - ₒdod-jo ('dod jo) v. jo-ba (jo ba). - ₒdod-dún ('dod dun) strong desire Cs. - ₒdod-dpál ('dod dpal) prop. n. Dodpál, a large hardware-manufactory and mint at the foot of the Potala in Lhasa. - ₒdod-brál ('dod bral), ₒdod-méd ('dod med), free from passions. - ₒdod-(pai) yón-(tan) ('dod (pai) yon (tan)) 'wished for goods', earthly goods and pleasures, whatever is grateful to the senses, such as ₒdód-pa lṅa ('dod pa lnga), a delight to the ears, the eyes, the palate etc. - ₒdod-lóg ('dod log) unchastity, lewdness, prostitution, spyód-pa (spyod pa) to have illicit, esp. incestuous intercourse, daṅ (dang) with. - ₒdód - sred - čan ('dod sred can) avaricious, greedy Pth., yet cf. čags - sred - čan (chags sred can); both words prob. signify the same. - ₒdod-lha ('dod lha) = ₒdod-pa ('dod pa) 5.
Lt., n. of a medicine (?) dkar (dkar), dmar (dmar), skyur-ₒdon (skyur 'don).

pf. bton (bton), fut. (Cs.) ydon (gdon), imp. ton (ton), W. *tón-če* (*ton che*), the vulg. word for ₒbyín-pa ('byin pa), vb. a. to tón-pa (thon pa), ₒbyún-ba ('byung ba), to cause to go out or to come forth, i.e. 1. to expel, throw out, eject, from the house, village etc.; to take out, from a box; to draw forth; to dig out, metals; *zán-ton-sa* (*zangs thon sa*) W. a copper-mine; *tón-te bór-če* (*ton te bor che*) W. to put, set, lay, place out; to let out, of prison Pth.; to drive or turn away, to dismiss, a servant, a wife etc., frq.; *ṅa k̇oi k̇a-ne čan ma ton* (*nga khoi kha ne can ma ton*) W. I could not get or force any thing out of him; mčí-ma ₒdón-pa (mchi ma 'don pa) to shed tears Glr.; with skad (skad) and similar words: to utter, to set up (a cry), to make one's self heard; hence 2. to pronounce, yi-ge γnyis-γnyis-su ₒdón-pa (yi ge gnyis gnyis su 'don pa) to pronounce two consonants as two distinct sounds Gram.; to pronounce a magic formula; klóg-pa daṅ ₒdón-pa-la góms-šiṅ (klog pa dang 'don pa la goms shing) practising reading and pronouncing Dzl.; to say, to repeat; to recite (sacred texts) with a singing, drawling tone, like that of mendicant friars; hence in general, to perform one's devotions; žal-ₒdón-du mdzád-pa (zhal 'don du mdzad pa), Tar. 95, 11, prob. resp. = k̇a-tón byéd-pa (kha ton byed pa) to repeat by heart; tŭgs-la ₒdón-pa (thugs la 'don pa) prob. to read silently. - 3 fig. to elevate, to raise, Kri tóg-tu (khri thog tu) Pth., or rgyál-sar (rgyal sar) Glr., to raise to the throne; mgo (mgo) v. mgo-ₒdon (mgo 'don), sub mgo (mgo) compounds; gžán-gyi srog (gzhan gyi srog) to prolong a person's life, by affording him a (scanty) subsistence Thgy.; *sróg-ton-Kan(-po)* (*srog ton mkhan (-po)*) W. the giver of life, ζωοποιός. - 4. *k̇a tón-če* (*kha ton che*) W. to sharpen a scythe by means of a hammer. - 5. to edit, to publish, books, Tar. 47, 17. - 6. čos mtá-ru ₒdón-pa (chos mtha' ru 'don pa) to arrive at the end and scope of religious knowledge Mil. - 7. W. resp. to take, to taste, to eat or to drink, don yín-na (don yin na) would you like a taste of that? dón-Kaṅ (don khang) dining-room; dón-gir (don gir) resp. for ta-gír (tha gir); dón-rag (don rag) for ă-rag (a rag).

1. to come together Lex., Lt. - 2. for ₒdám-pa ('dam pa) to choose, to make a choice Glr. - 3. also ₒdóms-pa ('doms pa), pf. γdams (gdams), ft. γdam (gdam), imp. ₒdoms ('doms), 1. to advise, cf. γdám-pa (gdam pa). 2. to exhort, bág-med-pa-rnams-tu (bag med pa rnams tu) wicked persons, brtsón-par (brtson par) to give diligence Tar. 3. to recommend Glr., to bid, to command, v. γdám-pa (gdam pa). - 4. Cs.: importance; business, occupation (?).
